<!-- Generated by the Analysis-tab state-of-the-art review workflow (run wf_7a526974-700), 2026-06-08. Review-only; no application code. -->

# review:design-craft

## Strengths to keep

These are genuinely well-executed and should be preserved, not "improved away":

- **Engine/UI separation is exemplary.** `useRunQuery` re-runs only on data-determining keys and treats `viz` as a pure reshape, so table↔chart switching never re-hits Supabase. The chart adapter (`vizAdapter.ts`) reads only the chart model, never raw facts. Honour invariant #6 in every recommendation below.
- **Token discipline is already high.** Nearly all of `ConfigRail`, `PivotTable`, `ResultChart`, `MonitoringView`, `MetricsModal`, `SaveViewModal`, `DrillPanel` use `var(--color-*)`, `var(--space-*)`, `var(--text-*)`, `var(--radius-*)`. The dual-axis logic in `ResultChart` (different units → left/right axis) and the de-DE comma formatting in `format.ts` are correct and on-brand.
- **Defensive empty/overload states already exist** in `ResultChart` (`Overload` with concrete "narrow it with a Filter / drop a Column / switch to Table" guidance) and `PivotTable` (`Empty`). This is better than most dashboards ship. Most of my empty-state work below is about *consistency*, not absence.
- **Density is correct for the audience.** 264px rail, `--text-caption`/`--text-label` sizing, tabular-nums, 0.5px borders. Do NOT "open up" spacing — EMOS is expert Fachsoftware. Reject any S-tier-checklist instinct toward generous whitespace, hero numbers, or marketing cards.

## Gaps vs state-of-the-art

### 1. Extract a shared `SegmentedControl` primitive; the rail re-implements segmented toggles three different ways
**Why it matters:** `ConfigRail` has a local `Segmented` (Scope, Normalize, View), but Compare is 5 stacked full-width `optionRow` buttons, Overlay is a one-off full-width toggle, and `AnalysisModule` hand-rolls the build/monitor toggle inline with literal `bg`/`border` ternaries. Four visually-distinct treatments for the same interaction ("pick one of N") is exactly the "vibe-coded patchwork" CLAUDE.md's design-system section warns against. There is no `SegmentedControl` anywhere in `src/components/ui/*` (confirmed by grep).
**Concrete change:** Add `src/components/ui/SegmentedControl.tsx` (export via the `ui` barrel). Props: `options: {id, label, hint?, icon?}[]`, `value`, `onChange`, `orientation?: 'horizontal' | 'vertical'` (Compare wants vertical/full-width; Scope/View want horizontal-wrap), `size?: 'sm'`. Internally a `role="radiogroup"` with `role="radio"` + `aria-checked` children (see #5). Replace the three `ConfigRail` usages, the Compare button stack, the Overlay toggle, and the build/monitor toggle in `AnalysisModule`. This collapses ~80 lines of bespoke styling into one tested primitive and is the single highest-leverage consistency win.
**Files:** new `src/components/ui/SegmentedControl.tsx`, `src/components/ui/index.ts`, `ConfigRail.tsx` (lines 86–98, 153–156, 231–250, 254–257, 262–276), `AnalysisModule.tsx` (lines 157–177).
**Impact:** H · **Effort:** M

### 2. Config-rail is a long ungrouped vertical scroll — add progressive disclosure
**Why it matters:** The rail renders Scope → Subjects → (Normalize) → Rows → Columns → Filters → Measures → Compare → View → Overlay top-to-bottom, every section always expanded. With several measures, filters, and a multi-athlete normalize block, the rail scrolls well past one viewport and the primary controls (Subjects, Measures) compete equally with rarely-touched ones (Overlay, custom date range). This is the "information hierarchy / progressive disclosure" gap from the design-principles checklist.
**Concrete change:** Make `Section` collapsible (header is a `<button aria-expanded>` with a chevron; persist open/closed per section to localStorage alongside saved views). Default-open: Scope, Subjects, Rows, Measures. Default-collapsed: Filters, Compare, View, Overlay (and show a one-line summary in the collapsed header, e.g. "Compare · Both", "Filters · 2"). Keep Normalize inline since it only appears in the multi-subject case and is contextually urgent. Do NOT add animation-heavy accordions — a 150ms height/opacity transition (tokens already define `--transition-base`) is the ceiling.
**Files:** `ConfigRail.tsx` `Section` component (lines 284–302) + a tiny `useCollapsedSections` helper next to `savedViews.ts`.
**Impact:** H · **Effort:** M

### 3. Plumb the coach's OWN stored colours through the engine for grouped series (the marquee chart win)
**Why it matters:** This is the most on-brand, most readable improvement and is currently *impossible in `ResultChart` alone*. I traced it: `FactRow` and `AnalysisResult`/`AnalysisMeta` (`types.ts`) carry **no colour**, and `factFetch.ts` selects `exercises(id,name,category,lift_slot,...)` and `macro_phases(id,name)` **without `color`** (lines 607–608, 646). So `vizAdapter.ts` falls back to a hardcoded generic `PALETTE` for every multi-series chart — even when the series ARE exercises/categories/phases/week-types that the coach has already colour-coded everywhere else in the planner and macro modules. A coach who sees "Snatch = teal" in the macro view sees it as palette-slot-blue here. Using their colours is both instantly readable and the kind of coherence that separates a real product from a generic dashboard.
**Concrete change (data-layer, not cosmetic):**
1. `factFetch.ts`: also select `exercises.color`, `macro_phases.color`, and the week-type / intensity-zone colours (week-type colours live in week-type config; intensity zones in `general_settings.intensity_zones`).
2. Emit `AnalysisMeta.dimensionColors: Partial<Record<Dimension, Record<string, string>>>` (value-label → hex) from `runAnalysisQuery`/`aggregate.ts`. This is a label→colour lookup, NOT per-fact colour, so it costs almost nothing and stays serializable.
3. `vizAdapter.ts`: when a series corresponds to a single coloured dimension (rows or cols is `exercise|category|meso|weekType|intensityZone`), resolve its colour from `dimensionColors` and only fall back to `PALETTE` for uncoloured dims. Keep the facet-colour logic (planned=muted gray, performed=accent) for the single-metric planned/performed case — that is intentional and correct.
This is explicitly sanctioned by CLAUDE.md's "Never tokenise data-driven or semantic colour" rule (phase/week-type/category colours are data, not chrome).
**Files:** `src/lib/analysis/types.ts` (`AnalysisMeta`, maybe `FactRow`), `factFetch.ts` (select + meta), `aggregate.ts`/`runAnalysisQuery.ts` (build the map), `vizAdapter.ts` (lines 29–60 consume it).
**Impact:** H · **Effort:** M (mostly data-layer; touches the engine, so coordinate with the engineering reviewer)

### 4. Table polish — sticky header, frozen first column, hover, optional zebra, in-cell value bars
**Why it matters:** `PivotTable` is the canonical accessible view and the default. Today it has no row hover, no sticky header (scroll a long week-by-week pivot and you lose the measure headers), and no frozen row-label column (scroll wide column-groups and you lose which exercise a row is). For a dense expert table these are table-stakes, and the design-principles "tables" guidance calls them out directly.
**Concrete change:**
- Wrap in a scroll container with `position: sticky; top: 0` on `<thead>` cells (`background: var(--color-bg-primary); z-index: 1`), and `position: sticky; left: 0` on the row-label `<td>`/`<th>` (the `tdLabel`/left `th`). Add a subtle right border on the frozen column for separation.
- Row hover: `tr:hover { background: var(--color-bg-secondary) }` — do this with a CSS class, not inline (inline can't express `:hover`). Add `.analysis-pivot tbody tr:hover` to a stylesheet.
- Optional **density toggle** (Comfortable/Compact) and optional **zebra** as a header control — but default to current density (it is correct); zebra OFF by default since 0.5px row borders already delineate.
- **In-cell value bars** for a single-measure numeric column: a faint `var(--color-accent-muted)` background bar scaled to the cell's share of the column max, behind right-aligned text. This is high-signal, low-chrome, and on-brand (it is a tokenised neutral tint, not data colour). Gate it to single-measure tables to avoid clutter.
**Files:** `PivotTable.tsx` (`th`, `tdLabel`, `tdValue`, the `<table>` wrapper), a small `.analysis-pivot` block in a stylesheet (or reuse `tokens.css` button-style pattern). Consider whether `DataTable` should absorb sticky-header support so the win is shared.
**Impact:** H · **Effort:** M

### 5. Accessibility (WCAG AA) — multiple concrete gaps
**Why it matters:** The builder is keyboard- and screen-reader-hostile in spots, and one series colour likely fails contrast. This is a checklist non-negotiable.
**Concrete change:**
- **`aria-pressed`/`aria-checked` missing on every toggle.** The `Segmented` buttons, Compare buttons, Overlay toggle, build/monitor toggle, and `FilterRow` value chips all convey selection purely by colour. Add `aria-pressed` (or `role="radio"`+`aria-checked` via the new `SegmentedControl`) so SRs announce state. Colour-only selection also fails 1.4.1 (use of colour).
- **Visible focus states.** `Button`/`.emos-btn` already has `:focus-visible { box-shadow: var(--focus-ring) }`, but the rail's raw `<button>`s, the `ExportItem`s, and the pivot cells (which are clickable `<td>`s with `onClick` but no keyboard path) have none. Make clickable cells real buttons or add `tabIndex={0}` + `onKeyDown` (Enter/Space) + a focus ring; the drill interaction is currently mouse-only.
- **Muted-gray planned series (`#888780` on white) is ~3.4:1** — fine as a 2px line stroke (graphical-object 3:1) but it doubles as legend text colour and fails 4.5:1 for the legend label. Keep the stroke, but render legend labels in `var(--color-text-secondary)` regardless of series colour (Recharts lets you style the legend text independently).
- **Chart a11y:** charts are inherently inaccessible to SRs; the *table view is the accessible fallback*. Make that explicit — add `role="img"` + an `aria-label` summary on each chart container ("Bar chart, planned vs performed tonnage by week"), and surface a visible "View as table" affordance from any chart (you already have the View segmented control; ensure Table is always reachable, including from the `Overload` and `Empty` states).
- **Export dropdown** (`AnalysisModule` lines 217–236) is a bare `position:absolute` div with no `role="menu"`, no Escape-to-close, no outside-click close, no focus trap. Convert to a small menu primitive or at minimum add Escape + click-outside.
**Files:** `ConfigRail.tsx`, `AnalysisModule.tsx`, `PivotTable.tsx`, `ResultChart.tsx` (Legend formatter + container aria), `SegmentedControl.tsx` (new).
**Impact:** H · **Effort:** M

### 6. Replace the bare `Spinner` with skeleton screens — and fix the Spinner's off-token colours
**Why it matters:** Loading today is a single `<Spinner/>` in the header (build mode), centred spinners in `DrillPanel`/`MonitoringView`. Design-principles IV calls for skeletons that preserve layout and reduce perceived latency. Separately, **`Spinner.tsx` is off-token**: `border-gray-200 border-t-blue-500` — raw Tailwind greys and `blue-500` (`#3B82F6`), which is neither the brand accent (`#185FA5`) nor a token. This is a literal off-brand tell flagged in CLAUDE.md.
**Concrete change:**
- Fix `Spinner.tsx` to use tokens: track `var(--color-border-tertiary)`, head `var(--color-accent)` (via arbitrary-value classes `border-[color:var(--color-border-tertiary)] border-t-[color:var(--color-accent)]`, mind the `color:` hint gotcha).
- Add lightweight skeletons: a `PivotTableSkeleton` (header row + 6–8 shimmer rows matching current row count if known) and a `ChartSkeleton` (axis frame + faint bars). Use the existing `--transition-*` for a subtle shimmer; keep it monochrome (`var(--color-bg-secondary)`), no gradient mesh. Show skeleton on first load / when subjects change; keep the header spinner only for fast re-query "refreshing" (stale-while-revalidate feel). MonitoringView's three panels especially benefit — it fires 3 queries and currently shows one spinner for all.
**Files:** `Spinner.tsx`, new `Skeleton.tsx` (or skeletons co-located in the builder), `AnalysisModule.tsx`, `DrillPanel.tsx`, `MonitoringView.tsx`.
**Impact:** M · **Effort:** M

### 7. Onboarding — presets as a card gallery, not a buried "Open…" dropdown
**Why it matters:** CLAUDE.md/UX-01 explicitly calls for "presets-first". Today the 5 well-described presets (`presets.ts` has `name` + `description`) are hidden inside a 190px "Open…" `<Select>` that shows only the name and stuffs the description into a `title` attribute no one hovers. On an empty/first-run analysis this is the discovery moment, and it's wasted.
**Concrete change:** When the result is empty or on a dedicated "Start" state, render a compact card gallery: each card shows `name`, `description`, and a tiny static preview (a 3–4 bar sparkline / mini-pivot thumbnail conveying the shape — line vs bars vs table). Keep it dense (2-up or 3-up grid, `var(--space-md)` gap, `var(--radius-md)` cards, hover = `var(--color-accent-border)`). The `<Select>` "Open…" stays for quick re-access once a coach knows the names, but the gallery is the first-run path. Saved views can appear as a second row of (deletable) cards. Do not build a wizard — a single card grid, no steps.
**Files:** new `PresetGallery.tsx`, `AnalysisModule.tsx` (the `!hasSubject`/empty branches, lines 104–113 and 253–258), reuse `presets.ts`.
**Impact:** M · **Effort:** M

### 8. Chart polish — compact unit-aware axis ticks, legend overflow, optional value labels, macro-phase ribbons
**Why it matters:** Axes currently render raw numbers (`<YAxis>` has no `tickFormatter`), so a tonnage axis shows `120000` not `120 t`, and the y-axis won't match the de-DE/`formatValue` formatting used in tooltips and cells — an inconsistency a numerate coach will notice. Legends overflow horizontally with many series (no wrap cap). And weekly charts have no macro context.
**Concrete change:**
- Add `tickFormatter` to every `<YAxis>` using a compact, unit-aware, de-DE formatter derived from `formatValue` (you already switch kg→t at 10 t in `format.ts`; reuse it: "12 t", "85 %", "1,8k reps"). The XAxis date ticks must use `dateUtils` `formatDateShort` (DD/MM), never a raw ISO or US format.
- Cap legend height and let it wrap/scroll (`wrapperStyle` with `maxHeight` + overflow), or move to a right-side legend for >~6 series; you already guard at MAX_SERIES=24, but 8–20 legible series still overflow one line.
- Optional **value labels** on bars (off by default; a header toggle) using `<LabelList>` with `formatValue` + `var(--color-text-secondary)` — useful for print/screenshots.
- **Macro-phase ribbons** behind weekly/dated charts: a thin band along the x-axis coloured by `macro_phases.color` (data-driven, sanctioned), so a tonnage curve reads against Prep/Comp/Transition. Implement as Recharts `<ReferenceArea>` spans (x1/x2 = phase boundaries) at low opacity, OR a CSS strip beneath the plot. This depends on #3's colour plumbing (phase colour + boundaries reaching the model).
**Files:** `ResultChart.tsx` (all `<YAxis>`/`<XAxis>`/`<Legend>`), `MonitoringView.tsx` (ACWR + adherence axes), `format.ts` (add a `formatAxisTick(value, unit)` compact variant), depends on `vizAdapter`/engine for phase spans.
**Impact:** M · **Effort:** M

### 9. Micro-interactions & transitions — purposeful, 150–300ms, currently inconsistent
**Why it matters:** Buttons transition `all 100ms`; the rail's raw segmented buttons and Compare rows have NO transition, so selection snaps. `pgrid` cells already use tasteful 100–150ms transitions — the analysis module should match that standard, not be the one rough surface.
**Concrete change:** Give the new `SegmentedControl`, FilterRow chips, and pivot cell hover a `transition: background var(--transition-base), color var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base)`. Drill-panel open/close and section collapse should ease at `--transition-slow` (200ms). Nothing decorative or bouncy — easing tokens already exist; just apply them. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (wrap shimmer/transition in the media query) for the a11y win.
**Files:** `SegmentedControl.tsx`, `ConfigRail.tsx` (FilterRow), `PivotTable.tsx`, the stylesheet block.
**Impact:** L · **Effort:** S

### 10. Empty / error state consistency
**Why it matters:** There are 4–5 near-identical local `Empty` components (`PivotTable`, `ResultChart`, `MonitoringView`, `HeatmapGrid`) with slightly different padding/sizing, plus error rendering that is a bare red `<div>` in `AnalysisModule`, `DrillPanel`, and (implicitly) the panels. Inconsistent empties read as unfinished.
**Concrete change:** Extract a shared `<EmptyState label icon? hint? action?>` and `<ErrorState message onRetry?>` (small, token-driven, optional lucide icon + optional CTA). Route every local `Empty` and the inline error blocks through them. Give error states a retry affordance (re-run the query) instead of a dead-end red line. The `Overload` state is good and specific — keep it, but have it render the shared empty's chrome with its custom body, and make its "switch to Table" suggestion an actual button (ties to #5 keyboard reachability).
**Files:** new `src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx` (+ `ErrorState`), `PivotTable.tsx`, `ResultChart.tsx`, `MonitoringView.tsx`, `HeatmapGrid.tsx`, `AnalysisModule.tsx`, `DrillPanel.tsx`.
**Impact:** M · **Effort:** S

### 11. Off-token / raw-hex usages to flag (small but real)
**Why it matters:** CLAUDE.md mandates flagging raw hex / off-token colour in new and touched code.
**Concrete change:**
- `vizAdapter.ts` `PALETTE` and `facetColor` use raw hex (`#185FA5`, `#888780`, …). These ARE the entity-palette 400-stops and ARE data-series colours, so per the "never tokenise data-driven colour" rule they're *defensible* — but the accent (`#185FA5`) duplicates `var(--color-accent)` and the muted gray duplicates `--color-gray-400`. Prefer reading these from the palette tokens (or a single exported `SERIES_PALETTE`) so a theme/accent change propagates. `GHOST_COLOR = '#B4B2A9'` = `--color-gray-200`; same treatment.
- `HeatmapGrid.tsx` hard-codes the accent ramp: `rgba(24, 95, 165, ...)` and `#fff`. The `24,95,165` is `--color-accent`'s RGB — if accent becomes user-configurable (tokens.css says it will), this heatmap won't follow. Drive it from the accent token (e.g. a single `--color-accent-rgb` token, or compute from the resolved accent). `#fff` text-on-dark-cell should be `var(--color-text-on-accent)`.
- `Spinner.tsx` `border-gray-200 border-t-blue-500` (covered in #6) — genuinely wrong, not data-driven.
**Files:** `vizAdapter.ts`, `HeatmapGrid.tsx` (lines 71–72), `Spinner.tsx`; consider adding `--color-accent-rgb` to `tokens.css`.
**Impact:** L · **Effort:** S

## Open questions

- **Colour-plumbing ownership (#3/#8):** these cross the engine boundary (`factFetch`/`aggregate`/`types`). Confirm with the engineering/domain reviewers that adding `dimensionColors` to `AnalysisMeta` is the agreed shape, and that selecting `color` in `factFetch` won't disturb the planned-vs-performed fact invariants. The UI half is cheap; the data half needs sign-off.
- **Density toggle (#4):** is a per-coach Comfortable/Compact toggle wanted at all, or does EMOS's "always dense" stance mean we ship Compact only and skip the toggle? My recommendation leans skip-the-toggle to avoid a setting that contradicts the product's identity — flag for the user.
- **Where does the preset gallery live (#7)?** Only on the empty state, or also as a persistent "New from preset" entry point? A gallery that only appears when empty is discoverable once; a coach who clears subjects may want it back.
- **Module is OUT OF SCOPE for shipping.** CLAUDE.md says Analysis must be hidden in the UI (nav/routes/entry points) while code stays intact. Every recommendation here is sound but should be implemented behind the existing hidden state — i.e., this is "ready for reactivation" polish, not work to surface the module now. Confirm the user wants design work invested in a currently-hidden module before the implementer spends effort.
- **Week-type & intensity-zone colours:** I confirmed exercise/category/phase colours exist in the DB; I did not verify that week-types and intensity-zones carry a stored colour (vs. being derived). Verify before promising those two series-colour mappings in #3.
